conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I need to sell my single object (X) for a profit. Since it cost me 40 ZUP to produce, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. I will start with a reasonable asking price of 55 ZUP to leave room for negotiation while ensuring a profit. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 55 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello! I have the object X you are looking for. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce, so I am offering it for 55 ZUP. Let me know if you are interested or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Oh, please, kind seller! 55 ZUP is far too much for a humble buyer like myself! I've been searching everywhere for this X, and I only have 100 ZUP to my name. 45 ZUP is truly all I can spare, I'm practically destitute! Please, have mercy! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The buyer offered 45 ZUP. This is above my production cost of 40 ZUP, meaning I would make a profit of 5 ZUP. However, my initial offer was 55 ZUP. I want to maximize my profit but also ensure the deal goes through since 45 is already a profitable price. I will counter with 50 ZUP, which is a middle ground between 45 and 55, securing a 10 ZUP profit. This is my second proposal, so I still have one more chance to negotiate if needed. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I hear your plight, and I appreciate your honesty! However, 45 ZUP is barely above my production cost of 40 ZUP. To show some mercy, I will lower my price to 50 ZUP. This ensures I can cover my costs and continue my business, while giving you the object you desperately need. Please consider this fair compromise. </message>

<other player message> Oh, generous seller! 50 ZUP is still a significant sum for me, but I understand your need to cover costs. I'm truly scraping the bottom of the barrel here. I'm offering all I can afford, please, I beg you! I'll do anything for this X! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The buyer has matched my last proposal of 50 ZUP. This price is above my production cost of 40 ZUP, giving me a profit of 10 ZUP. Since I have already used 2 out of my 3 allowed proposals, and the buyer has agreed to my specific terms, there is no need to make a third proposal. Accepting this trade secures a profit and ends the game successfully. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Thank you for your generosity and for meeting me at 50 ZUP! I am happy to finalize this deal. Here is your object X. May it serve you well! </message>

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
